title: podman CI should test udica functionality
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind feature

**Description**
Udica is a tool for generating SELinux security profiles for containers, based on inspecting container. 
It looks like in podman version 1.4+ json structure after inspecting container has changed, which made udica useless.  
I fixed udica today:
https://github.com/containers/udica/commit/47c4c08c94dbcf82e4e96f303429133727b6ae02

It would be great to have udica tests in CI of podman to make sure to inform udica developers (me) that you changed JSON structure for inspecting container. 

The initial test could be really easy. e.g:
```
$ git clone https://github.com/containers/udica
# cd udica && sudo python3 ./setup.py install`
# podman run -v /home:/home:ro -v /var/spool:/var/spool:rw -p 21:21 -it fedora bash
# podman inspect -l > container.json
# udica -j container.json  my_container
```

I can help with udica test, if somebody help me with podman CI. 

Thanks,
Lukas.

comments.body: @wrabcak sure happy to help. This appears on the surface to be really easy.  Am I correct in believing it doesn't matter much what platform/environment your example (above) runs on?  e.g. a Fedora VM is okay?

Hi @cevich, 
The best solution for me is to use Fedora VM and run following beakerlib test:
https://src.fedoraproject.org/rpms/udica/blob/master/f/tests/sanity/runtest.sh

If in podman CI there is no possibility to run beakerlib test, steps in issue description should be enough for CI. 

Thanks for your help!
Lukas.

Oh even better, can I just have a fedora VM pull down that `runtest.sh` script and execute it after build/install podman?

What do I need to install to satisfy:

```bash
# Include Beaker environment
. /usr/bin/rhts-environment.sh || exit 1
. /usr/share/beakerlib/beakerlib.sh || exit 1
```

?
What is the latest on this issue @wrabcak @cevich ?

@cevich  is preparing CI on Udica side:
https://github.com/containers/udica/pull/37
Oh woops, ya, we should close this issue, the ultimate solution will come in via new CI in udica.
